title: Exposing Pod Metadata via the Downward API
description:
## Exposing Pod Metadata via the Downward API

The **Downward API** lets a container access information **about itself** — its own name, namespace, labels, resource limits — without querying the Kubernetes API. This is useful for self-identifying logging agents, admission webhooks, and sidecar proxies.

Two delivery mechanisms:
- **Environment variables** — using `fieldRef` or `resourceFieldRef`
- **Files** — using a `downwardAPI` volume

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `self-aware` using `busybox:1.36` with command `sleep 3600` that exposes the following as **environment variables**:

| Env Var Name | Source |
|---|---|
| `MY_POD_NAME` | `metadata.name` |
| `MY_POD_NAMESPACE` | `metadata.namespace` |
| `MY_POD_IP` | `status.podIP` |

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl exec self-aware -- env | grep MY_POD
```

hints.title: Using fieldRef in env vars
hints.body: Use env[].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath to reference a pod's own metadata field.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: self-aware
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    env:
    - name: MY_POD_NAME
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          fieldPath: metadata.name
    - name: MY_POD_NAMESPACE
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          fieldPath: metadata.namespace
    - name: MY_POD_IP
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          fieldPath: status.podIP
EOF

commands.description: MY_POD_NAME env var uses fieldRef metadata.name
MY_POD_NAMESPACE env var uses fieldRef metadata.namespace
MY_POD_NAME resolves to 'self-aware' inside the container
